actionUnits.condition: Room_Small currently shows only a Transform component (fileID 2799994661978644995) with Has_Rigidbody: False and no visible MonoBehaviour/Collider/EventTrigger in the provided snippet. To proceed, we will trigger UnityEvent-based interactions defined on any MonoBehaviour(s) attached to Room_Small or its children once their exact scene-serialized settings (including m_Calls) are provided. Please supply the MonoBehaviour components (script fileIDs, method names, and m_Calls) and any Collider/EventTrigger presence for Room_Small and its listed children so we can target specific events. Note: The MonoBehaviour entries in the scene file will be treated as the exact script settings for test execution.

MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID 1053131476719109107, guid 064a8dd92b340ea44b0dfedc7cf8e7b8) is the Hourglass script and MonoBehaviour_1 (fileID 3259508153452503771, guid 35ffe483d6934704b884767450fd49e5) is XROffsetGrabbable. These MonoBehaviour components are the exact serialized settings of their scripts. XROffsetGrabbable UnityEvents (m_OnSelectEnter, m_OnSelectExit, etc.) have empty m_Calls, so there are no persistent UnityEvent callbacks to trigger directly. To cover XROffsetGrabbable OnSelectEntered/OnSelectExited paths, we need the XR Interactor GameObject(s) fileID(s) (e.g., XRDirectInteractor) to perform a Grab and Release on Hourglass.

DoorAnimationEvent is serialized as MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID 1635398365, guid a8a2c5194e4c1f1458096e7bee47220e) on Door 1 and this scene meta is the exact settings of the attached script. BoxCollider (fileID 1635398364) has m_IsTrigger=1, so OnTriggerEnter will be raised when a valid Collider enters. Simulate a Collider entering Door 1's trigger to execute OnTriggerEnter, which calls openDoors() and Animator.SetTrigger('DoorTrigger').

With the same serialized settings, simulate a Collider exiting Door 1's trigger to execute OnTriggerExit. The script invokes closeDoors() after 0.5 seconds, which triggers Animator.SetTrigger('DoorTrigger') again to close. Allow at least 0.5s for the Invoke to fire.

MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID 353440281, guid a8a2c5194e4c1f1458096e7bee47220e) is DoorAnimationEvent and MonoBehaviour_1 (fileID 353440283, guid 3dc13bc17e982d54e848fac3441c08c4) is AudioTrigger. These MonoBehaviour components are the exact serialized settings of their scripts on Door 2. BoxCollider (fileID 353440280) has m_IsTrigger=1. Simulate a Collider entering the trigger to execute: DoorAnimationEvent.OnTriggerEnter -> openDoors() -> Animator.SetTrigger('DoorTrigger'); and AudioTrigger.OnTriggerEnter -> audioSource.Play() once due to oneTimeEvent=1.

Simulate a Collider exiting the BoxCollider (trigger) to execute DoorAnimationEvent.OnTriggerExit while isOpened=true; this schedules Invoke('closeDoors', 0.5f). Wait at least 0.5s to allow closeDoors() to run and trigger Animator.SetTrigger('DoorTrigger') to close.

To cover AudioTrigger.Update branch (stopping audio when keysPlaced.numKeys == 3), set keyMainScript (fileID 2046914207) numKeys to 3 before Update runs, then let one frame elapse so AudioTrigger.Update calls audioSource.Stop() on AudioSource (fileID 1941851346). Please provide how to set or simulate keysPlaced.numKeys=3 (public method/event or scene override).
